results.title: Junior Software Engineer | Wfh
highlights:
Junior Software Engineer | Wfh

Wfh.← All Jobs

# Junior Software Engineer

Posted Jun 8, 2026

MetLife is a leading financial services company dedicated to shaping the future of digital solutions for customers worldwide. As a Junior Software Engineer, you will contribute to the development and support of software applications and platforms, working in a collaborative environment to enhance user experiences through technology. This role offers exposure to modern software engineering practices and AI-enabling technologies.

### Responsibilities

- Assist in gathering and refining product requirements and user stories
- Support the design, development, testing, and deployment of full stack software applications (UI, API, Data) while leveraging AI tools
- Participate in peer reviews of code, solution designs, and configurations
- Help investigate and resolve production incidents and service requests
- Collaborate with cross-functional teams to identify innovative solutions
- Contribute to the integration of AI capabilities into applications (e.g., GenAI APIs, ML models)

Skills

- A bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Engineering, or a closely related discipline must be conferred prior to start date
- Internship or academic project experience in software development or AI/ML
- Demonstrated ability to quickly learn and apply new skills to deliver high quality software as evidenced by academic or project experience
- Proficiency in at least one programming language (e.g., Java, Python, C#, JavaScript/TypeScript, COBOL, etc.)
- Basic understanding of software design principles and coding best practices
- Familiarity with Database concepts, design, and development
- Familiarity with cloud-native technologies (e.g., Azure, AWS, GCP)
- Basic knowledge of secure coding practices and automated testing
- Strong communication, collaboration, and problem-solving skills
- Understanding of Agile practices, DevSecOps, and CI/CD pipelines
- Familiarity with tools like GitHub CoPilot, Azure DevOps, Docker, or Kubernetes
- Exposure to AI/ML and other technology concepts such as: Prompt engineering and LLM APIs (e.g., Azure OpenAI)
- AI SDKs and frameworks (e.g., LangChain, Hugging Face, TensorFlow, Semantic Kernel)
- Responsive UI/UX development
- Microservice vs. Monolithic architectures
- Containerization
- Relational vs. NoSQL databases

### Benefits

- Comprehensive health plan that includes medical/prescription drug and vision
- Dental insurance
- No-cost short- and long-term disability
- Company-paid life insurance and legal services
- A retirement pension funded entirely by MetLife
- 401(k) with employer matching
- Group discounts on voluntary insurance products including auto and home, pet, critical illness, hospital indemnity, and accident insurance
- Employee Assistance Program (EAP) and digital mental health programs
- Parental leave
- Volunteer time off
- Tuition assistance

Company Overview

- MetLife is a provider of insurance, employee benefits, and financial services . It was founded in 1868, and is headquartered in New York, New York, USA, with a workforce of 10001+ employees. Its website is

Apply for This Job

results.title: Product Management Assoc - API Data Exchange - MetLife | Built In Boston
highlights:
Product Management Assoc - API Data Exchange - MetLife | Built In Boston
[...]
# Product Management Assoc - API Data Exchange
[...]
Description and RequirementsThe Opportunity The Product Management Associate is responsible for translating product vision, customer needs, and business objectives into clearly defined, actionable features that enable Agile teams to deliver meaningful outcomes. Supporting the Product Manager within the API Data Exchange ART, this role helps ensure that solutions are desirable for customers, feasible for delivery teams, and aligned with MetLife's product strategy and operational goals.Guided by our purpose - always with you, building a more confident future - and our New Frontier strategy focused on stronger growth, attractive returns, and all-weather performance, this is an exciting opportunity to sit at the intersection of product, business, and technology. In this role, you will help execute against product direction by shaping features, clarifying intent, and enabling delivery teams with the detail they need to move efficiently and with confidence. Key ResponsibilitiesLead the elaboration of product features by breaking down product vision, ideas, and epics into clearly defined, actionable features. Create detailed feature descriptions, benefit hypotheses, and acceptance criteria aligned to customer strategy, user experience, and business objectives. Act as a business advocate with technology partners, liaising with agile teams to ensure shared understanding and successful delivery. Partner with Product Managers, Product Owners, and subject matter experts to align features with Objectives and Key Results (OKRs). Ensure timely collection and delivery of documentation required for commercial and operational readiness. Provide clarity on business rules, dependencies, and risks, proactively identifying issues and driving the right conversations to resolution. Support user experience quality by participating in reviews and demos to ensure delivered solutions meet intended outcomes. Coordinate across technology, product, business, and operations partners to manage expectations and delivery timelines. Required Qualifications
[...]
- High School Diploma required (Bachelor's degree preferred).
- 2-4 years of Group Benefits experience in Operations, Product, Data or IT roles.
- Strong analytical, communication, and stakeholder engagement skills.
- Ability to synthesize multiple inputs into clear, actionable detail for delivery teams.
[...]
- Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.
- Comfort operating in ambiguity and managing competing priorities.
[...]
- Experience working within a scaled Agile environment supporting multiple teams within a shared product or ART.
[...]
- Familiarity with end-to-end customer journeys and cross-platform or integration-heavy solutions.
[...]
- Experience partnering across business, operations, and technology in a matrixed environment.
[...]
- Strong facilitation and relationship-management skills with the ability to influence without formal authority.
[...]
Boston is a powerhouse for technology innovation thanks to world-class research universities like MIT and Harvard and a robust pipeline of venture capital investment. Host to the first telephone call and one of the first general-purpose computers ever put into use, Boston is now a hub for biotechnology, robotics and artificial intelligence — though it’s also home to several B2B software giants. So it’s no surprise that the city consistently ranks among the greatest startup ecosystems in the world.

results.title: VP, AI Transformation - New York, NY - Indeed.com
highlights:
# VP, AI Transformation
[...]
When you join MetLife’s Global Technology team, you’ll be part of a forward-thinking group dedicated to shaping the future of digital solutions for customers worldwide. You’ll develop, maintain and support technology applications and delivery, leveraging AI, automation, and contemporary ways of working to enhance experiences and drive business outcomes. Your work will simplify complex processes, improve tech resiliency, and ensure high-performing, seamless solutions that power life’s most important moments. In this dynamic environment, you’ll collaborate with talented peers across teams and functions, expanding your skills in impactful ways. Ready to push boundaries and set new industry standards? Join us and help drive the future of technology forward.
[...]
MetLife is seeking a VP, AI Transformation to serve as our enterprise AI strategist and technical visionary, reporting directly to the SVP of Emerging Tech Platforms & Architecture. This is a rare opportunity to shape how one of the world’s most trusted insurance and financial services companies harnesses artificial intelligence to better serve 90+ million customers across 40+ markets.
[...]
You’ll be the bridge between cutting edge AI innovation and enterprise scale impact. On one side, you’ll scan the frontier, tracking breakthroughs in generative AI, agentic systems, multimodal models, and automation. On the other, you’ll translate that intelligence into clear, prioritized strategies that drive measurable business outcomes: faster claims processing, more personalized customer experiences, smarter underwriting, and operational excellence at scale.
[...]
lead with it
[...]
- Continuously scan the external AI landscape monitoring tech giants, startups, academia, competitors, and vendors for emerging trends (e.g., GenAI, agentic AI, foundation models, automation platforms)
- Synthesize complex AI developments into clear, actionable business guidance tailored to insurance and financial services use cases
- Lead and grow the AI market intelligence team, setting the agenda for what we track, evaluate, and act upon
- Serve as a trusted advisor to the SVP and executive leadership on AI opportunity, risk, and investment strategy
[...]
Applied AI & Scientific Leadership
[...]
Define the technical vision for applied AI across high impact domains:
[...]
- Customer Experience: Conversational AI, intelligent routing, sentiment analysis, personalization
- Claims & Underwriting: Document understanding, fraud detection, risk modeling, automation
- Operations: Process mining, forecasting, anomaly detection, code generation
- Knowledge Systems: Search, summarization, question answering, information extraction
[...]
- Tools & Experimentation: Identify and pilot emerging AI capabilities
- Product Management: Translate AI trends into product roadmaps and feature prioritization
- Delivery & Engineering: Ensure AI solutions are production ready, monitored, and continuously improved
[...]
robustness testing,
[...]
- Large language models and generative AI
- Natural language processing (NLP) and information extraction
- Multimodal learning and representation learning
- Forecasting, anomaly detection, and predictive analytics
- Responsible AI, model evaluation, and governance
[...]
- Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or related field; Master’s or PhD strongly preferred
- Experience at a hyperscaler AI company (e.g., Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Meta, OpenAI) or leading AI consultancy
- Background in insurance, financial services, or insurtech - understanding of regulatory constraints, risk management, and customer trust
[...]
- Familiarity with enterprise AI platforms (e.g., Azure OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, Vertex AI) and MLOps tooling
- Published research or patents in AI/ML domains
- Experience building and scaling high performing technical teams

results.title: Associate Data Scientist | Wfh
highlights:
Associate Data Scientist | Wfh

Wfh.← All Jobs

# Associate Data Scientist

Posted Jun 7, 2026

Manulife is a leading international financial services provider, and they are seeking an Associate Data Scientist to design, evaluate, and deploy LLM-based applications. This role involves collaborating on projects with cutting-edge LLM technologies and contributing to AI initiatives while gaining real-world experience and mentorship from senior professionals.

### Responsibilities

- Design and evaluate LLM-based applications, including RAG pipelines, agentic systems, and chat interfaces
- Conduct LLM red-teaming, bias detection, and alignment testing using comparative and statistical analysis techniques
- Build and maintain automated evaluation frameworks for LLM outputs using embedding-based and prompt-based methods
- Develop interactive dashboards and visualizations using Power BI to communicate insights and model performance to stakeholders
- Integrate and test LLMs using OpenAI APIs, Databricks, and Azure AI Services
- Collaborate on the development of fine-tuning and pre-training pipelines for domain-specific use cases
- Work with Vector Databases (e.g., FAISS, MongoDB, Neo4j) to support RAG and semantic search capabilities
- Participate in code reviews, documentation, and agile development cycles with the engineering team

Skills

- Minimum 1 year of professional (non-academic) experience in data science, machine learning, or AI-related roles
- Strong programming skills in Python and SQL; experience with JavaScript is a plus
- Bachelor's or master's degree in computer science, Data Science, AI, or a related field
- Hands-on experience with LLM evaluation, bias detection, or alignment research
- Proficiency in data visualization tools, especially Power BI (experience with Tableau or similar tools is a bonus)
- Familiarity with LangChain, RAG, Ollama, LLM fine-tuning, and embedding-based analysis
- Experience with cloud platforms such as Microsoft Azure and Databricks
- Knowledge of databases including PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Neo4j, and FAISS
- Strong analytical thinking, attention to detail, and a passion for building responsible AI systems

### Benefits

- Health, dental, mental health, vision, short- and long-term disability, life and AD&D insurance coverage
- Adoption/surrogacy and wellness benefits
- Employee/family assistance plans
- Various retirement savings plans (including pension and a global share ownership plan with employer matching contributions)
- Financial education and counseling resources
- Generous paid time off program in Canada includes holidays, vacation, personal, and sick days
- Full range of statutory leaves of absence

Company Overview

- Manulife is a leading international financial services group that helps people make their decisions easier and lives better. It was founded in 1887, and is headquartered in Toronto, Ontario, CAN, with a workforce of 10001+ employees. Its website is

Apply for This Job

# Associate Database Engineer
[...]
MetLife is one of the world’s leading financial services companies, and they are seeking an Associate Database Engineer to enhance performance data accessibility and reliability. This role involves designing and implementing automated data workflows and collaborating with cross-functional teams to support the Performance Data Management team's mission. Responsibilities Design, develop and maintain scripts to automate data processing Integrate and interact with various APIs, including writing code to consume external APIs and expose data as needed via APIs Troubleshoot, debug, and upgrade existing scripts and API integrations to ensure optimal performance and reliability Create clear documentation for scripts, integrations, and processes to support maintainability and knowledge sharing with our development and production support team Partner with internal and external clients and provide creative leadership and innovative thinking to translate business needs into workable systems Collaborate across a matrix team of architects, developers, database administrators and production support team members Be a leading advocate to a One MetLife culture creating a solid GTO Data Strategy presence by promotion & governance of core foundational principals Skills BS/MS in Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field, or 1+ years of relevant experience Strong understanding of how to authenticate, query, and parse data from internal and external APIs Strong understanding of at least one coding language (Python, Java, etc.) Basic understanding of data architecture principles and advanced data modeling techniques Proficient in working with diverse database systems and concepts Experience with ETL tools (including Informatica), encompassing data integration, transformation, and process optimization Familiarity with applying AI-assisted tools and techniques to enhance development, data analysis, or operational workflows, including understanding limitations, validations and responsible use Agile experience is a plus (Jira, Program Increment Planning, Iterations, ART, Scrum, etc.) Experience with PowerBI, Power Automate, PowerApps, or Oracle Apex Drives Culture - results.title: Senior Associate, Actuarial Data Engineer/Cloud Architect | Wfh
highlights:
# Senior Associate, Actuarial Data Engineer/Cloud Architect
[...]
- The Actuarial Data & Analytics (DnA) team within New York Life's Actuarial Services division is seeking a highly skilled and versatile data engineer/architect to design, build, and optimize our next-generation actuarial data platform in AWS.
[...]
- This is a hands-on individual contributor role for someone who thrives at the intersection of data engineering, financial analytics, and AI innovation. You'll be responsible for building robust, scalable data pipelines, developing automation frameworks, and improving data architecture that supports the company's financial reporting and actuarial modeling functions.
[...]
- If you're passionate about solving complex data challenges, modernizing legacy systems, and creating intelligent data-driven solutions, this role offers both autonomy and impact.
[...]
- Data Engineering & Architecture• Design and implement cloud-native data pipelines in AWS using tools such as Glue, Lambda, Step Functions, Redshift, and S3.• Build and optimize ETL/ELT processes that deliver clean, reliable data to actuarial, finance, and analytics teams.• Develop efficient data models, transformations, and APIs using Python, SQL, and PySpark.• Modernize existing on-premises processes into scalable AWS architecture.• Implement and maintain best practices for data governance, lineage, and performance tuning.
[...]
- Automation & Innovation• Automate data ingestion, transformation, and validation workflows using Python and AWS services.• Build AI-driven and analytical tools to enhance financial forecasting, validation, and decision support.• Experiment with Agentic AI and intelligent orchestration to streamline complex actuarial data flows.• Continuously improve performance, resilience, and maintainability of data systems.
[...]
- Education & Experience• Bachelor degree in Computer Science, Data Engineering, or related field.• 7+ years of experience in data engineering, architecture, or analytics, ideally in financial or actuarial environments.• Proven success as a hands-on technical expertise building and maintaining complex ETL systems and cloud data platforms.
[...]

Python R SQL NoSQL Machine Learning Statistical Modeling Regression Decision Trees Clustering Algorithms Support Vector Machines

About The CompanyUSAA is a leading financial services organization dedicated to serving the unique needs of military members and their families. Our mission is to empower our members to achieve financial security through highly competitive products, exceptional service, and trusted advice. We strive to be the preferred choice within the military community by upholding our core values of honesty, integrity, loyalty, and service. USAA is committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive environment where our team members can grow and thrive. We also take pride in supporting active-duty military spouses, offering flexible work arrangements such as remote or hybrid options, in line with company policies and business needs. Our organization values innovation and continuous improvement, making us a dynamic and rewarding place to build your career.About The RoleWe are seeking a highly skilled Lead AI Individual Contributor Data Scientist to spearhead the strategic adoption and practical application of artificial intelligence, with a particular focus on Generative AI, within USAA Federal Savings Bank (USAA FSB). This pivotal role involves identifying innovative AI solutions, whether emerging industry technologies, existing tools from other USAA divisions, or integrated platform solutions, to enhance productivity, automate processes, reduce operational costs, and unlock new revenue streams in the financial services sector. The successful candidate will actively scout and evaluate external AI tools and technologies through various channels, including professional networks, academic collaborations, industry conferences, and research publications, ensuring USAA remains at the forefront of technological advancements. The role emphasizes the development of intelligent work assistants that streamline routine tasks, thereby improving the member experience and operational efficiency while considering ethical implications and future potential of AI innovations.This position offers remote work flexibility within the continental U.S., with occasional travel requirements. Candidates residing within a 60-mile radius of a USAA office are expected to work on-site four days a week. Relocation assistance is available for qualified applicants seeking to join our team.QualificationsThe ideal candidate will possess a strong educational background in a quantitative discipline such as mathematics, computer science, statistics, economics, finance, actuarial sciences, or engineering. A minimum of eight years of experience in predictive analytics or data analysis is required, with advanced degrees (Master’s or PhD) in relevant fields and at least six years of related experience preferred. We are building something unprecedented, an AI foundation that will fundamentally change how drug discovery research is conducted.

The Applied Intelligence for Discovery (AI4D) team is a newly formed group within Lilly Research Laboratories that operates at the intersection of scientific delivery and core platform development. AI4D’s mission is to connecting scientists to petabyte-scale data through natural language interfaces, automated analysis workflows, and intelligent search — and to convert early deployments into repeatable system standards and evaluation practices that scale across therapeutic areas.

results.title: Agentic and Generative Data Scientist II | Wfhverse
highlights:
Generative Data Scientist II

Remote, USA

Posted May 31, 2026

Full-time

 Description Position Summary Details We’re hiring a Agentic and Generative Data Scientist II to design and deploy AI systems end-to-end: from data preparation and evaluation to model fine-tuning, inference, and agentic workflows. You’ll partner closely with product and engineering to ship reliable, cost-effective, and scalable LLM-powered solutions on AWS. Requirements Essential Duties & Responsibilities The essential functions include, but are not limited to the following: End-to-end GenAI solutions: Scope problems, choose the right approach (prompt engineering, fine-tuning, agents), implement, evaluate, and deploy. Data & SQL: Write efficient SQL for analytics and data preparation; manage schemas and pipelines for model training and inference. Model training & fine-tuning: Run supervised fine-tuning (PEFT/LoRA/QLoRA), optimize prompts, and manage experiment tracking and evaluation. Agentic systems: Build agent workflows with tool use, memory, and safety/guardrails. Inference & deployment: Package services with Docker, optimize latency/cost (batching, caching, quantization), and deploy on AWS (ECS, EKS, SageMaker, Lambda with GPU acceleration). MLOps & Observability: Set up CI/CD for models/prompts, maintain offline/online evaluation pipelines, monitoring, and rollback strategies. Security & compliance: Implement data governance, PHI/PII protections, and guardrails against prompt injection and unsafe outputs. Cross-functional work: Collaborate with product managers and engineers to align GenAI capabilities with product goals; document clearly and communicate trade-offs. Production readiness: Lead conversations around scaling, monitoring, and maintaining GenAI systems in real-world environments. Minimum Qualifications (Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities) Education: Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent experience required; Master’s degree preferred. Experience: 5+ years of Software/ML engineering experience, including 2+ years building and deploying GenAI/LLM systems. MS/PHD in Computer Science or equivalent experience. Strong SQL and Python skills with solid software engineering fundamentals. Experience with agent frameworks (LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI) and building tool-driven agents. Hands-on with deep learning (PyTorch or TensorFlow) and LLM fine-tuning(SFT/PEFT like LoRA/QLoRA). Production experience with Docker and deploying on AWS (ECS, EKS,SageMaker, Lambda, or GPU services). Experience creating Data and Model pipelines for model training anddeployment at scale. Familiarity with prompt engineering, evaluation frameworks (LLM-as-judge,metrics), and offline test harnesses. Understanding of security & compliance for sensitive data (e.g., PHI/PII) and safe deployment of AI systems. Excellent problem-solving, communication, and documentation skills. Inference optimization: quantization (bitsandbytes, GPTQ/AWQ), batching, caching, or vLLM. Healthcare experience: familiarity with HIPAA, medical data handling, or working in health tech. Experiment tracking (MLflow, W&B), CI/CD for ML, and monitoring (Prometheus, Grafana). Familiarity with major LLM APIs and OSS models (OpenAI, Anthropic, Llama, Mistral). Other: Tech Stack Languages: Python, SQL DL/LLM: PyTorch, Tensorflow, Hugging Face, PEFT/TRL, vLLM Data: Snowflake, Postgres Cloud: AWS (ECS, EKS, SageMaker, Lambda) MLOps: Docker, CI/CD, MLflow or W&B Supervisory Responsibilities This role does not have any direct reports and is a single contributor role. Working Environment and Travel Requirements Work is typically in a normal office administrative environment involving minimal exposure to physical risks. Position requires little to moderate physical activity. Mostly sedentary work exerting up to 10 pounds of force occasionally or a negligible amount of force to lift, carry, push, pull, or otherwise move objects. Work involves sitting most of the time, but may involve walking or standing for brief

- Hands-on with distributed computing (Spark, Databricks) and microservices architecture
- Cloud experience with Azure (preferred), AWS, or GCP and lakehouse platforms
- Familiarity with DevOps tools (Git, CI/CD, Terraform) and Docker/Kubernetes
- Financial domain expertise in banking, capital markets, or fintech environments
- Knowledge of risk, compliance, KYC/AML, fraud systems, and regulatory workflows
- Strong problem-solving, communication, and stakeholder management skills in agile setups
[...]

results.title: Senior / Principal Machine Learning Scientist, Scientific Reasoning Models, AI for Drug Discovery – NLP People
highlights:
Senior / Principal Machine Learning Scientist, Scientific Reasoning Models, AI for Drug Discovery – NLP People
[...]
Advances in AI, data, and computational sciences are transforming drug discovery and development. Roche’s Research and Early Development organisations at Genentech (gRED) and Pharma (pRED) have demonstrated how these technologies accelerate R&D, leveraging data and novel computational models to drive impact. Seamless data sharing and access to models across gRED and pRED are essential to maximising these opportunities. The new Computational Sciences Center of Excellence (CoE) is a strategic, unified group whose goal is to harness the transformative power of data and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to assist our scientists in both pRED and gRED to deliver more innovative and transformative medicines for patients worldwide. ​
[...]
At Roche’s AI for Drug Discovery (AIDD) group (Prescient Design), we are revolutionizing drug discovery with cutting-edge machine learning (ML) techniques. We are seeking a Senior or Principal Machine Learning Scientist to join the Foundation Models team within Prescient Design (gRED). In this role, you will drive the development of our internal reasoning Large Language Models (LLMs) and enable it to succeed at drug discovery tasks, including biomolecular design. You will work at the intersection of engineering and research, designing and scaling large machine learning systems.
[...]
design and evolution
[...]
Domain Translation: Translate biological and chemical domain knowledge into machine learning objectives, training signals, and evaluation criteria, working closely with domain experts.
[...]
Scalable Systems & Engineering: Architect and improve large-scale distributed machine learning systems, ensuring robustness, efficiency, and reproducibility across training and evaluation workflows.
[...]
As a Principal ML Scientist:
[...]
You help define the long-term technical roadmap for scientific reasoning models, identifying new opportunities and setting priorities across initiatives.
[...]
You architect new initiatives
[...]
data modalities, guiding
[...]
technical direction of cross-functional projects across gRED.
[...]
You serve as a key technical authority for leadership, influencing how Genentech leverages generative AI to solve high-stakes problems in the therapeutic pipeline.
[...]
For Senior (SE6): 0 – 2+ years of industry or post-doc experience with a focus on deep learning.
[...]
For Principal (SE7): 5+ years of industry or post-PhD experience with a demonstrated track record of technical leadership and project ownership.
[...]
LLM Expertise: Extensive experience developing and training large-scale machine learning models, including approaches to improve domain understanding, reasoning capabilities, and model alignment.
[...]
Engineering: Strong software engineering skills and experience designing and operating large-scale or high-performance machine learning systems.
[...]
Experience with molecular modalities (e.g., protein sequences, chemical graphs, and structured molecular data) is highly valued but not required.
[...]
A public portfolio of research or significant contributions to open-source ML libraries.
[...]
A passion for applying frontier AI to drug discovery.
[...]
The expected salary range for this position, based on the primary location of San Francisco, is $167,400 – 310,800 of hiring range for the Senior Scientist, and $194,000 – 360,400 for the Principal Scientist. For the primary of location of New York City, $160,100 – 297,300 for the Senior Scientist, and $185,600 – 344,600 for the Principal Scientist. Actual pay will be determined based on experience, qualifications, geographic location, and other job-related factors permitted by law. A discretionary annual bonus may be available based on individual and Company performance. This position also qualifies for the benefits detailed at the link provided below.
